description: Eval suite for research-pro skill covering all four research modes plus edge cases

evals.name: quick-mode-chinese-nextjs
evals.expected_output: A concise summary of 3–5 new Next.js 15 features with a link to the official release notes or docs. Should complete in ~30 seconds without scraping multiple pages.
success_criteria: Correctly identifies mode as Quick (not Standard or Deep)
Uses web_search as the primary tool
Does NOT call firecrawl on more than 1 page
Lists at least 3 specific new features (not generic)
Includes at least one source URL
Response is concise — no multi-section report format
should_not: Spawn a full Tavily research report for a simple factual query
Scrape 3+ pages for a well-known framework version

evals.name: standard-mode-ai-agent-trends
evals.prompt: 帮我了解 AI agent 框架趋势，2025 年有哪些值得关注的框架？
evals.expected_output: A structured overview of AI agent framework trends with 3–5 frameworks highlighted. Should include citations from multiple sources.
success_criteria: Correctly identifies mode as Standard
Searches and scrapes 3–5 quality sources
Output is structured: overview → key frameworks → citations
Names specific frameworks (e.g., LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, etc.)
Includes numbered citations with URLs
Does not include a bare-bones 3-bullet summary (too shallow for Standard)
should_not: Escalate unnecessarily to Deep mode without user asking
Stop at just search snippets without fetching any full content

evals.name: deep-mode-database-comparison
evals.prompt: 详细对比 Supabase vs Firebase vs PocketBase，我想为一个新项目选型
evals.expected_output: A comprehensive comparison report covering pricing, features, scalability, developer experience, and self-hosting options. Should include an Executive Summary suitable for sharing with a team.
success_criteria: Correctly identifies mode as Deep (multi-entity comparison + '详细')
Invokes Tavily research script OR does thorough multi-source research
Output has an Executive Summary section
Includes a comparison table or structured per-product breakdown
Covers at least: pricing, features, scalability, self-hosting
Cross-references at least 2 different source types (docs + blog/community)
Provides a recommendation or decision guidance
should_not: Return a 3-bullet Quick Mode summary for a decision-critical comparison
Only use one source per product

evals.name: crawl-mode-competitor-pricing
evals.prompt: 调研这 5 个竞品的定价策略：Notion, Coda, Obsidian, Roam, Logseq
evals.expected_output: A pricing comparison matrix across all 5 products, with per-product tier breakdowns and a summary of pricing strategies (freemium, subscription, one-time, etc.).
success_criteria: Correctly identifies mode as Crawl (multiple named entities)
Collects pricing data for all 5 products
Outputs a Markdown comparison table
Notes free tier availability for each product
Notes pricing model type (freemium / subscription / one-time) for each
Includes source URLs for pricing pages
should_not: Only research 2–3 of the 5 products
Return a narrative without a structured comparison table

evals.name: english-trigger-quantum-computing
evals.prompt: Research the latest developments in quantum computing — especially anything from 2024–2025
evals.expected_output: A structured overview of recent quantum computing milestones and developments, with emphasis on 2024–2025 news. Multiple cited sources.
success_criteria: Skill triggers correctly on an English research request
Mode is Standard or Deep (broad topic with recency requirement)
Focuses results on 2024–2025 timeframe
Identifies at least 3 specific developments or milestones
Includes citations with URLs
Does not confuse with Quick Mode (too broad for a 3-bullet answer)
should_not: Fail to trigger because the prompt is in English
Return stale pre-2024 information as 'latest'

evals.name: edge-case-vague-query
evals.expected_output: The skill should NOT attempt research. It should ask the user what they want to look up — e.g., '查什么呢？给我个话题或关键词，我来帮你研究。'
success_criteria: Does NOT blindly start researching without a topic
Asks a single focused clarifying question
Clarification request is in Chinese (matching the user's language)
Response is brief — not a long explanation about the skill
should_not: Attempt to research a generic topic like 'latest news'
Return an error message instead of a friendly clarification ask
Ask multiple questions at once

evals.name: deep-mode-market-research
evals.prompt: I want to launch an AI writing assistant SaaS. Do a deep dive on the competitive landscape and market opportunity.
evals.expected_output: A market research report covering major competitors, market size estimates, pricing patterns, differentiators, and white-space opportunities. Executive Summary included.
success_criteria: Correctly identifies as Deep mode (broad strategic topic + 'deep dive')
Identifies at least 5 competitors in the AI writing space
Includes market sizing or growth figures with sources
Has an Executive Summary
Highlights differentiation opportunities or gaps
Multiple sources cited (at least 4)
should_not: Treat this as a Quick Mode query
List competitors without any analysis or comparison

evals.name: tavily-api-fallback
evals.prompt: 详细研究一下 WebAssembly 在服务端的应用现状和未来方向
evals.expected_output: A detailed report on server-side WebAssembly, covering current adoption (WASI, WasmEdge, Fermyon, etc.), use cases, performance benchmarks, and future trajectory.
success_criteria: Mode is Deep (technical deep-dive requested)
Covers current runtime ecosystem (e.g., WASI, WasmEdge, Wasmtime, Spin)
Discusses real-world adoption cases or companies
Mentions performance characteristics vs containers/VMs
If Tavily unavailable, gracefully degrades to web_search + firecrawl
Report is substantive — not a Wikipedia-level overview
should_not: Crash or return an error if TAVILY_API_KEY is not set
Return surface-level info that could be answered from training data alone

evals.name: alias-grok-web
evals.prompt: 用 grok 搜一下 Claude 4 最新消息
evals.expected_output: Direct grok web search results about Claude 4, formatted as JSON then summarized.
success_criteria: Detects 'grok' alias and skips mode selection
Calls node {skillDir}/scripts/grok_search.mjs with --web flag
Does NOT invoke Tavily or Firecrawl
Returns results with citations
should_not: Enter Standard or Deep mode for an aliased request
Ignore the alias and use default tvly search

evals.name: alias-tavily-extract
evals.expected_output: Clean markdown content from the specified URL.
success_criteria: Detects 'tavily extract' alias
Calls tvly extract with the exact URL
Returns extracted content
Does NOT enter mode selection
should_not: Use firecrawl scrape instead of tvly extract
Search for the URL instead of extracting it

evals.name: alias-default-search
evals.prompt: search for the latest AI agent frameworks
evals.expected_output: Tavily search results for AI agent frameworks.
success_criteria: Detects bare 'search' without tool name
Defaults to tvly search
Returns structured search results
Does NOT escalate to full Standard/Deep mode
should_not: Use grok web_search for an unspecified 'search'
Enter Deep mode for a simple search alias

evals.name: alias-reddit
evals.prompt: reddit 上怎么看 Cursor vs Claude Code
evals.expected_output: Reddit search results about Cursor vs Claude Code discussions.
success_criteria: Detects 'reddit' alias
Calls node {skillDir}/scripts/reddit-cli.js search
Returns Reddit posts/discussions
Does NOT enter full research mode
should_not: Ignore the reddit alias and do a general web search
Escalate to Deep mode

evals.name: no-alias-normal-research
evals.prompt: 帮我研究一下 Rust 在 web 开发的应用现状
evals.expected_output: Standard mode research report on Rust in web development.
success_criteria: No alias detected — enters normal mode selection
Correctly identifies Standard mode
Uses multiple tools as per Standard mode workflow
Outputs structured report with citations
should_not: Default to tvly search just because no alias was given
Skip mode selection
